name: Caesar Salad
tagline: Crisp romaine, garlic-anchovy dressing, parmesan, garlic croutons
highlight: Real caesar — anchovy, garlic, lemon — no nut-laced shortcuts

hero.alt: Caesar salad with romaine spears, parmesan, and toasted croutons
hero.caption: caesar — garlic and anchovy

healthFacts: Anchovies are tiny but rich in omega-3s and bioavailable B12.
Romaine is hydrating — about 95% water — and high in vitamin K.

steps.title: Make croutons
steps.detail: Toss bread cubes in olive oil, salt, and a smashed garlic clove. Bake 425°F for 8 minutes, tossing once, until crisp and gold.
steps.tip: Stale bread = better croutons. Day-old bread takes oil more evenly.

steps.title: Mash the dressing base
steps.detail: In a bowl, mash garlic and anchovies into a paste with the side of a knife. Whisk in egg yolk, lemon juice, and Dijon.
steps.tip: Anchovies dissolve into umami — even anchovy haters won't taste them.

steps.title: Drizzle and emulsify
steps.detail: Slowly drizzle olive oil while whisking until thick and glossy. Stir in half the parmesan. Taste, adjust salt and acid.
steps.tip: Add oil too fast and it breaks. Slow at first, then thicker stream.

steps.title: Toss and shower
steps.detail: Tear romaine into bite-sized pieces. Toss with dressing and croutons. Shower with remaining parmesan and cracked pepper.
steps.tip: Dress just before eating — Caesar wilts within 10 minutes.
